old_claim.note: Spectacles (Eyeglasses) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The medieval spectacle trade created the concrete lens-grinding and assembly demand base for optical workshops; it is enabling craft context rather than a prerequisite for every later optical instrument.

source_shape.source_locator: Early chapters summarize the late-thirteenth-century Italian invention and diffusion of spectacles and connect spectacle making to the grinding and polishing of lenses in Venice and Florence.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source links spectacles to an emerging craft industry for grinding, polishing, and assembling optical lenses in northern Italian production centers.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because this node is scoped to workshops rather than the first eyeglass artifact, the spectacle trade is the concrete demand and craft channel that supports the workshop edge.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: optical_workshops keeps spectacles_eyeglasses as an enabling craft-market edge with source-backed scope.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to pre-spectacle optical experimentation only.
A stronger source shows medieval optical workshops did not emerge from spectacle and corrective-lens production.
A separate spectacle_workshops node is added and should replace the broader spectacles_eyeglasses prerequisite.
short_reason: Spectacle making supplied the workshop craft context.
